title: roldana hartwegii var. durangensis
intro: Roldana hartwegii var. durangensis, also known as Mexican Mountain Sage, is a perennial plant native to the mountainous regions of Mexico. It is a member of the Asteraceae family, valued for its medicinal properties, including anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial compounds. The plant's leaves and flowers have been used to treat various health conditions, including digestive issues and respiratory problems, due to its expectorant and antispasmodic properties, offering benefits such as reduced inflammation and improved immune function. In herbal preparations, the plant's leaves and flowers are often dried and used in teas, infusions, and tinctures, as well as being added to soups and salads for its culinary and medicinal value.
